title: Kangaroo Level 3-4 - Practice Set 14: Problem Solving
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: What is the sum of 27 and 18?
How many small squares are shown in the grid?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 120 80' width='120' height='80'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='30' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='50' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='10' y='30' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='30' y='30' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='50' y='30' width='20' height='20'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A grid of 2 rows and 3 columns of small squares.
questions.solution: There are 2 rows of 3 squares, so 2 x 3 = 6.

questions.question_text: A movie starts at 3:15 and lasts 45 minutes. When does it end?
What number comes next in the pattern: 2, 5, 8, 11, ___?
A pencil costs 30 cents. How much do 4 pencils cost?
Which shape below has exactly 3 sides?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 60' width='200' height='60'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='20,50 40,10 60,50'/><rect x='80' y='15' width='30' height='30'/><circle cx='145' cy='30' r='15'/><polygon points='175,10 195,25 185,50 165,50 155,25'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Four shapes in a row: a triangle, a square, a circle, and a pentagon.

questions.question_text: What is 60 divided by 5?
A ribbon is 100 cm long. Mia cuts off 35 cm. How long is the remaining ribbon?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 220 40' width='220' height='40'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='15' width='200' height='10'/><line x1='80' y1='5' x2='80' y2='35' stroke-dasharray='3,3'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A long ribbon with a dashed line indicating a cut.

questions.question_text: Ana, Ben, and Cal sit in a row. Ana is not first. Cal is last. Who is in the middle?
questions.solution: Cal is last; Ana is not first, so Ana is in the middle and Ben is first.

questions.question_text: How many triangles are in the figure below?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 120 100' width='120' height='100'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='60,10 10,90 110,90'/><line x1='60' y1='10' x2='60' y2='90'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A large triangle with a vertical line from the top vertex to the base, creating two smaller triangles.
questions.solution: Two small triangles plus the whole large triangle gives 3 total.

questions.question_text: A box contains 48 marbles. If they are shared equally among 6 children, how many marbles does each child get?
A pizza is cut into 8 equal slices. Leo eats 3 slices. What fraction of the pizza is left?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 100 100' width='100' height='100'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='50' cy='50' r='40'/><line x1='50' y1='10' x2='50' y2='90'/><line x1='10' y1='50' x2='90' y2='50'/><line x1='22' y1='22' x2='78' y2='78'/><line x1='78' y1='22' x2='22' y2='78'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A pizza divided into 8 equal slices.

questions.question_text: In the sequence of shapes circle, square, triangle, circle, square, triangle, ..., what is the 14th shape?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 40' width='200' height='40'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='20' cy='20' r='10'/><rect x='45' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><polygon points='90,30 100,10 110,30'/><circle cx='140' cy='20' r='10'/><rect x='165' y='10' width='20' height='20'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A repeating pattern of circle, square, triangle.
questions.solution: The pattern repeats every 3. 14 / 3 = 4 remainder 2, so the 14th shape is the 2nd in the pattern: square.

questions.question_text: Tom practices piano for 25 minutes every day. How many minutes does he practice in one week?
Sara has $5. She buys a book for $2.75. How much change does she get?
A rectangle has length 8 cm and width 3 cm. What is its perimeter?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 120 60' width='120' height='60'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='20' y='15' width='80' height='30'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A rectangle labeled with length 8 cm and width 3 cm.

questions.question_text: Five friends each shake hands with every other friend exactly once. How many handshakes happen in total?
questions.solution: Each of the 5 people shakes hands with 4 others; total pairs = 5 x 4 / 2 = 10.

questions.question_text: How many small cubes were used to build the stack shown?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 140 120' width='140' height='120'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='20' y='70' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='50' y='70' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='80' y='70' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='20' y='40' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='50' y='40' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='20' y='10' width='30' height='30'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A stair-step stack of cubes: 3 on the bottom, 2 in the middle, 1 on top.

questions.question_text: The sum of three consecutive whole numbers is 36. What is the largest of them?
questions.solution: The middle number is 36/3 = 12, so the numbers are 11, 12, 13. Largest is 13.

questions.question_text: In a farm there are chickens and cows. Together they have 8 heads and 22 legs. How many cows are there?
questions.solution: If all 8 were chickens, legs would be 16. Each cow adds 2 more legs. 22 - 16 = 6, so 6/2 = 3 cows.

questions.question_text: The numbers 1, 4, 9, 16, 25, ... follow a pattern of square numbers. What is the 8th number in this sequence?
A square is folded in half twice, making a smaller square. If the original side length was 12 cm, what is the side length of the resulting square?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 160 80' width='160' height='80'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='60' height='60'/><line x1='40' y1='10' x2='40' y2='70' stroke-dasharray='3,3'/><line x1='10' y1='40' x2='70' y2='40' stroke-dasharray='3,3'/><rect x='100' y='25' width='30' height='30'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A square showing dashed fold lines, next to a smaller resulting square.
questions.solution: Folding in half once gives 12/2 = 6, but folding in half twice (one along each axis) gives a square with side 12/2 = 6 cm.

questions.question_text: Anya has twice as many stickers as Ben. Together they have 27 stickers. How many does Ben have?
questions.solution: Let Ben have x, Anya 2x. Then 3x = 27, so x = 9.

questions.question_text: Four cards numbered 1, 2, 3, 4 are placed in a row. The 2 is not at either end, and the 4 is to the right of the 1. Which of the following could NOT be the arrangement?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 180 50' width='180' height='50'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='50' y='10' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='90' y='10' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='130' y='10' width='30' height='30'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Four empty cards shown in a row, to be filled with digits.
questions.solution: In option D, 4 is to the left of 1, which violates the rule that 4 must be to the right of 1.
